Configure up to three SunEnergyXT heads: Configure up to three SunEnergyXT heads managed by this instance. Head 1 is required; heads 2 and 3 are optional (leave the host empty if unused). Each must be reachable via its local HTTP API (local mode LM=1). For multiple heads put them on different phases — this is the operator's responsibility; the adapter regulates the net (summed) grid power.
Device self-regulation works with a sing: Device self-regulation works with a single head only. Remove head 2/3, or choose the adapter controller.
Enable if your meter uses >0 = feed-in /: Enable if your meter uses >0 = feed-in / <0 = draw.
Foreign state holding the grid power of: Foreign state holding the grid power of your house meter (e.g. a Shelly Pro 3EM total). Convention: >0 = grid draw, <0 = feed-in.
Log a warning if the grid source has no: Log a warning if the grid source has no update for this long.
Meter serial number for Shelly/Tasmota (: Meter serial number for Shelly/Tasmota (mDNS); the LAN IP for EcoTracker (direct). For Tasmota use the SN prefix without the last 4 characters.
Minimum change of a head's setpoint befo: Minimum change of a head's setpoint before it is re-written (anti-chatter). Keep it small — too high relative to the gain leaves small grid deviations uncorrected (effective grid dead zone ≈ this ÷ gain).
Required. Local IP or hostname of the fi: Required. Local IP or hostname of the first SunEnergyXT head (local mode LM=1 must be enabled).
Set GS to 0 (neutral) on all heads if th: Set GS to 0 (neutral) on all heads if the grid source is stale for this long.
The adapter binds this meter into the (s: The adapter binds this meter into the (single) storage (MM=1 + MD) and lets the device regulate itself; it does not write GS. Only the four meter types below are supported, and the meter must be reachable by the storage on the local network. Note: the meter stays usable in ioBroker (it is only polled over HTTP).
The control modes are mutually exclusive: The control modes are mutually exclusive; the adapter enforces the matching device mode (MM) on every head and warns if it is changed externally. Requires local mode (LM=1) on each device.
The controller computes one total setpoi: The controller computes one total setpoint from your grid-power meter (feed-forward + P) and splits it equally across the online heads, capped per head with overflow; a full head (charging) or empty head (discharging) drops out. The adapter forces MM=0 on every head so the devices execute GS.
This host is already used by another hea: This host is already used by another head.
Your Tasmota / BitShake energy-monitor s: Your Tasmota / BitShake energy-monitor subtype (from the device API reference, section 5.4). The adapter derives the correct power key (dat_str.pwr) from it.
• Adapter controller — ioBroker writes: •  Adapter controller — ioBroker writes the grid setpoint (GS) from any meter state and splits it across all heads; works with ANY meter and with 1–3 heads.
• Device self-regulation — bind a suppo: •  Device self-regulation — bind a supported meter and let the storage regulate itself (single head only).
• Off — monitoring only: the adapter ne: •  Off — monitoring only: the adapter never writes MM / MD / GS.
⚠ Device self-regulation works with a s: ⚠  Device self-regulation works with a single head only. With more than one head configured this mode is ignored (the adapter stays in monitoring/off). Use the adapter controller for multiple heads.
